title: How Cannabis Can Trick Your Memory
summary: A new study shows that cannabis, a drug from the marijuana plant, can make people remember things that never happened. Scientists at Washington State University tested 120 people who use cannabis regularly. They gave some people a fake version and others real cannabis with THC, the chemical that makes you feel high. After taking it, the people did memory tests for about an hour. The tests checked different types of memory, like remembering words or where information came from. People who had THC did worse on most tests. They often said they heard words that were never said and had trouble remembering where they learned things. This means cannabis can create false memories and confuse your memory, which might be a problem in important situations like remembering what you saw as a witness. The study found that even a little THC can affect memory a lot, and it doesn't matter if you take more or less.

options: They can confuse important memories like eyewitness reports
questions.correct_answer: They can confuse important memories like eyewitness reports

background_read: Cannabis is a plant that people sometimes use as a drug. It contains THC, which can make you feel relaxed or high. Memory is how our brains store and recall information, like what we did yesterday. Scientists study memory to understand how drugs like cannabis affect it. This research helps us learn about safety and health.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists at Washington State University and 120 regular cannabis users
WHAT: A study showing cannabis with THC can create false memories and confuse memory
WHERE: At Washington State University in a controlled experiment
WHY: To understand how cannabis affects different types of memory and why it might be risky in real-life situations

perspectives.description: Cannabis broadly disrupts memory systems, leading to false memories and source confusion, which could have serious implications.
